questions.q: Samudragupta was an emperor of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Samudragupta was a 4th-century CE emperor of the Gupta dynasty.

questions.q: By what modern nickname is Samudragupta often known because of his conquests?
questions.explanation: His wide-ranging military campaigns earned Samudragupta the nickname the 'Indian Napoleon'.

questions.q: Samudragupta's campaigns are mainly recorded in an inscription on a pillar at which place?
questions.explanation: The Allahabad (Prayag) pillar inscription is the chief record of Samudragupta's conquests.

questions.q: Whose son was Samudragupta?
questions.explanation: Samudragupta was the son and successor of Chandragupta I, founder of Gupta imperial power.

questions.q: Besides being a conqueror, Samudragupta was a patron of the arts and personally skilled as a?
questions.explanation: Samudragupta was an accomplished musician, even depicted playing the veena on his coins.

questions.q: In which century CE did Samudragupta reign?
questions.explanation: Samudragupta reigned in the 4th century CE, roughly the middle decades.

questions.q: Samudragupta extended Gupta influence across much of which broad region?
questions.explanation: Through conquest and alliances Samudragupta spread Gupta power across much of the subcontinent.

questions.q: Which musical instrument is shown on Samudragupta's coins, reflecting his artistic side?
questions.explanation: A famous Gupta coin type shows Samudragupta seated and playing the veena.

questions.q: Samudragupta succeeded which ruler on the Gupta throne?
questions.explanation: Samudragupta succeeded his father Chandragupta I, who had first taken imperial titles.

questions.q: Which kind of ruler best describes Samudragupta in Indian history?
options: A conquering emperor and patron of culture
questions.explanation: Samudragupta combined extensive conquest with patronage of arts and learning.

questions.q: Who composed the eulogy (prashasti) of Samudragupta on the Allahabad pillar?
questions.explanation: The court poet Harishena composed the Sanskrit prashasti praising Samudragupta's deeds.

questions.q: Samudragupta's Allahabad inscription was engraved on a pillar originally erected by which earlier emperor?
questions.explanation: Samudragupta's eulogy was added to a pillar that had first carried the edicts of the Mauryan emperor Ashoka.

questions.q: Towards the southern kings he defeated, Samudragupta followed a policy described as which?
questions.explanation: In the Dakshinapatha campaign Samudragupta captured southern kings and then reinstated them, a policy of grahana-moksha-anugraha.

questions.q: Samudragupta is said to have revived which ancient Vedic royal ritual to proclaim his sovereignty?
questions.explanation: Samudragupta performed the Ashvamedha, commemorated by special coins, to assert paramount sovereignty.

questions.q: Which coin type, depicting a horse before a sacrificial post, celebrates Samudragupta's revival of a Vedic rite?
questions.explanation: The Ashvamedha-type coins, showing a horse at the yupa (sacrificial post), mark Samudragupta's horse sacrifice.

questions.q: The Allahabad inscription lists kings of the north whom Samudragupta did what to?
questions.explanation: In Aryavarta (the north) Samudragupta is said to have forcibly uprooted rival kings and annexed their lands, unlike his southern policy.

questions.q: Samudragupta's father, who had married into the Lichchhavi clan, was?
questions.explanation: Samudragupta's father was Chandragupta I, whose Lichchhavi marriage strengthened the dynasty.

questions.q: Samudragupta is praised in the inscription with the epithet 'Kaviraja', meaning?
questions.explanation: The title Kaviraja, 'king among poets', celebrates Samudragupta's literary and artistic talents.

questions.q: Who is generally regarded as the son and successor of Samudragupta?
questions.explanation: Samudragupta was succeeded by Chandragupta II Vikramaditya, who expanded the empire further.

questions.q: The Allahabad prashasti records that many frontier states and tribal republics did what towards Samudragupta?
options: Paid tribute and accepted his suzerainty
questions.explanation: Frontier kingdoms and forest tribes are described as submitting, paying tribute and obeying Samudragupta's commands.

questions.q: Samudragupta is credited with extensive conquest combined with what cultural quality, making him a model 'dharmic' emperor?
options: Patronage of learning and the arts
questions.explanation: Beyond conquest, Samudragupta's patronage of scholars, poets and music shaped his image as an ideal ruler.

questions.q: The Allahabad pillar inscription is composed in which language?
questions.explanation: Harishena's prashasti for Samudragupta is written in elegant classical Sanskrit.

questions.q: Roughly how many kings of Dakshinapatha (the south) does the Allahabad inscription claim Samudragupta defeated and reinstated?
questions.explanation: The inscription names about twelve southern kings, such as those of Kosala and Kanchi, whom Samudragupta captured and released.

questions.q: One distant ruler of Kanchi defeated in Samudragupta's southern campaign is named in the inscription as?
questions.explanation: Vishnugopa of Kanchi is among the Dakshinapatha kings listed as defeated by Samudragupta.

questions.q: The Allahabad inscription mentions a foreign king who sought Samudragupta's favour and is identified with the ruler of which island?
questions.explanation: The inscription notes that the Simhala (Sri Lankan) king Meghavarna sent envoys seeking Samudragupta's permission to build a monastery at Bodh Gaya.

questions.q: Samudragupta's gold coin types are remarkably varied; which type shows him with a battle-axe?
questions.explanation: Among Samudragupta's coin types is the Battle-axe (Parashu) type, alongside the Standard, Archer, Tiger-slayer, Ashvamedha and Lyrist types.

questions.q: The Eran inscription, which records the name of Samudragupta, is located in which present-day state?
questions.explanation: The Eran stone inscription bearing Samudragupta's name is in Sagar district of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: The Allahabad inscription claims that even rulers of distant outlying regions, including the Shakas and which other foreign kings, paid homage?
questions.explanation: The inscription says the Shakas and the Kushana 'Daivaputra-Shahi-Shahanushahi' rulers sought to please Samudragupta and accept his authority.

questions.q: Samudragupta's policy in the north (Aryavarta) differed from the south chiefly in that he?
options: Directly annexed northern kingdoms but only subdued southern ones
questions.explanation: Samudragupta annexed defeated Aryavarta kingdoms into the empire, whereas in the south he reinstated rulers as tributaries.

questions.q: The Sri Lankan king who sought Samudragupta's permission for a monastery at Bodh Gaya is traditionally named?
questions.explanation: Tradition names Meghavarna of Sri Lanka as the king who obtained Samudragupta's leave to found a monastery for pilgrims at Bodh Gaya.
